title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a weekend art class.
audio_script: Woman: Good afternoon, and welcome to the information line for our weekend art class. I’m going to give you the main details before the course begins. The class is for adults who enjoy drawing but have not studied art before. It will take place at [14]Hill College[/14], which is opposite the park and easy to reach by bus. Please don’t go to the town gallery, where we held the course last year. Your teacher will be Daniel Reed, who usually paints landscapes. On Saturday morning, he will start with simple work using [15]pencils[/15], so please bring a set if you have one. We can lend you some, but there may not be enough for everyone. The class begins at nine forty-five, but please arrive by [16]9:30[/16] because the college doors are locked after that. During the day, you’ll work in a large room on the second floor. There is a lift, so carrying your art bag should not be a problem. Lunch is not included in the price. However, the college [17]café[/17] will be open, and it sells sandwiches, soup and hot drinks. In the afternoon, students will go outside and draw buildings near the [18]river[/18], if the weather is dry. If it rains, Daniel will bring photographs to work from instead. At the end of Sunday’s lesson, there will be a small display of students’ pictures. Friends can come to see it, and entry is [19]free[/19]. Please phone again if you need directions or want to change your booking. If you are travelling by car, use the side entrance to the college, because the front car park is closed for repairs this weekend.

questions.explanation: The class will take place at Hill College.
On Saturday morning, students will start using pencils.
Lunch will be available in the college café.
Students will draw buildings near the river.
Entry to the final display is free.
